grant_and_term.reserved_rights_scope: Extremely broad — franchisor retains right to operate, franchise, acquire/merge competitive businesses, use alternative distribution channels, service corporate accounts, and engage in any activity not expressly prohibited, all within franchisee's territory
grant_and_term.system_modification: Franchisor may modify all components of the System in its Reasonable Business Judgment; franchisee must promptly comply; modifications shall not materially alter fundamental rights

fee_obligations.royalty: Greater of 5% of Gross Sales (weekly) or Minimum Weekly Royalty ($144–$1,010/week per territory)
fee_obligations.royalty_out_of_territory: 10% of Gross Sales for Open Area customers
fee_obligations.contact_center_fee: Up to 3% of Gross Sales (monthly) — Article 5.D. explicitly caps at 3%
fee_obligations.payment_method: Mandatory ACH authorization — franchisor debits automatically
fee_obligations.payment_priority: Franchisor may apply payments first to interest, then non-compliance fees, then oldest obligations (Article 5.G.)
fee_obligations.withholding_prohibited: Franchisee may not withhold payments for any reason (Article 5.H.)

site_selection_construction.office_requirement: Administrative Office within Operating Territory, approved by franchisor
site_selection_construction.home_office: Permitted with franchisor's written approval in sole discretion
site_selection_construction.site_deadline: 60 days from signing to secure approved location
site_selection_construction.failure_consequence: Termination with 30-day cure period; no refund of fees

training_obligations.replacement_managers: Must complete initial training at $500 per manager
training_obligations.supplemental_training: $500/day plus travel expenses if required by franchisor
training_obligations.system_wide_training: Up to 5 days per calendar year if franchisor establishes program
training_obligations.annual_conference: Mandatory attendance (up to 5 days/year, $1,000 fee per attendee)

operational_obligations.approved_services_only: May only offer Approved Services and Products
operational_obligations.business_management_system: Must use franchisor-designated BMS; franchisor has direct live access to all data
operational_obligations.data_ownership: All Business Management System Data accessible by franchisor
operational_obligations.digital_media_control: All digital/social media must be approved; must assign accounts to franchisor
operational_obligations.telephone_assignment: Must assign all telephone listings to franchisor
operational_obligations.pricing: Franchisee sets prices except for corporate accounts negotiated by franchisor
operational_obligations.insurance_requirements: Comprehensive coverage required (GL $1M/$2M, umbrella $2.5M, cyber $1M, EPLI $1M, etc.)
operational_obligations.inspections: Franchisor may inspect without prior notice during business hours; mystery shopper programs authorized
operational_obligations.audit_rights: Franchisor may audit books/records without notice; if underreporting ≥2%, franchisee pays audit costs

non_curable_immediate: 3+ curable defaults (even if each was cured)
Intentional/knowing refusal to comply with agreement or manuals
Failure to complete training to franchisor's satisfaction
Foreclosure action not dismissed after 60 days
Failure to continuously own and operate
10_day_cure: Failure to pay fees/obligations to franchisor or affiliates
Failure to pay supplier without legal justification
30_day_cure: Any other default not covered by immediate or 10-day provisions
Failure to open by Scheduled Opening Date
Failure to comply with operational standards
Failure to comply with system standards

transfer_restrictions.consent_required: Prior written consent, granted or withheld in franchisor's discretion
conditions: Not in default of any agreement with franchisor
General release by franchisee, owners, and spouses
Franchisor must waive right of first refusal
transfer_restrictions.rofr: Franchisor has 30 days to match any bona fide offer; 60 additional days to close
transfer_restrictions.rofr_cash_substitution: Franchisor may substitute cash for any payment form in the offer
transfer_restrictions.assignment_to_corporate_entity: Permitted if franchisee retains majority control and managing interest

noncompete.during_term: No involvement in any Competitive Business
noncompete.post_term_scope: Operating Territory + 25-mile radius + any other Home Halo territory
noncompete.competitive_business_definition: Any non-medical at-home care or home healthcare agency/business providing seniors/veterans with daily living assistance, hands-on care, skilled care, nursing, therapy, or social worker services
noncompete.injunctive_relief: Franchisor may obtain injunction without bond, upon due notice

guaranty_scope.who_must_guarantee: All Owners and their respective Spouses
guaranty_scope.scope: Individually, jointly, and severally guarantee ALL obligations under Franchise Agreement
guaranty_scope.includes: Financial obligations, confidentiality covenants, non-competition covenants
guaranty_scope.personal_liability: Unlimited — all marital and personal assets at risk
guaranty_scope.exhibit: Exhibit 1 — Owner and Spouse Agreement and Guaranty (pages 143–160)

liquidated_damages.note: No explicit liquidated damages clause found in the Franchise Agreement. However, North Dakota addendum states 'no consent to termination or liquidated damages shall be required' — implying the concept may be addressed in the Owner/Spouse Agreement or in practice.

dispute_resolution.mediation_cost_split: 50/50 for mediator/AAA fees; each party bears own costs
dispute_resolution.governing_rules: AAA Commercial Dispute Rules + Federal Arbitration Act
dispute_resolution.exceptions: Franchisor may elect to skip mediation for (a) injunctive relief claims and (b) payment disputes
dispute_resolution.limitations_period: 1 year from discovery or 2 years from act/omission, whichever earlier (except payment, restrictive covenant, and IP claims by franchisor)
dispute_resolution.jury_waiver: Yes — both parties waive right to jury trial
dispute_resolution.class_action_waiver: Yes — both parties waive right to class action or consolidated proceedings

indemnification.franchisee_indemnifies_franchisor: For any and all losses, liabilities, claims, actions, damages, costs, and expenses arising from or related to the operations of the Franchised Business
indemnification.franchisor_indemnifies_franchisee: For direct damages from trademark infringement (if franchisee used marks as authorized, timely notified, gave sole control of defense, and was in compliance)

insurance_requirements.property: 100% of property value, special form perils (flood/earthquake if applicable)
insurance_requirements.general_liability: $1M occurrence / $2M aggregate (occurrence basis, contractual liability, products/completed ops, personal/advertising injury)
insurance_requirements.business_interruption: 12 months net income including Royalty Fees
insurance_requirements.additional_insured: Franchisor, officers, directors, shareholders, partners, agents, representatives, independent contractors
insurance_requirements.notice_of_cancellation: 30 days prior written notice to franchisor
insurance_requirements.certificate_deadline: Earlier of 90 days after Effective Date or prior to initial training

force_majeure.note: No explicit force majeure clause identified in the Franchise Agreement.

distinctive_clauses: Customer review non-compliance fee ($250–$750/month) triggered by ratings below 4.5/5 or 90% approval — unique penalty structure
All digital media accounts must be assigned to franchisor — complete control of online presence
Business Management System Data is accessible by franchisor with 'direct live access' — no data privacy firewall
Contact Center is exclusive to franchisor if established — creates mandatory vendor dependency with up to 3% of Gross Sales cost
Open Area service at 10% royalty rate (double the standard 5%) — penalty for serving customers outside territory
Three or more cured defaults trigger non-curable termination — ratchet provision
Payment application priority — franchisor applies payments to interest and penalties first, then oldest debts
Franchisor may substitute cash for any payment form in ROFR match — advantageous to franchisor
